Description: DEBIAS-M is described as a domain-adaptation framework for inference and correction of processing bias in microbiome studies.
MetaDICT estimates microbiome batch effects with covariate balancing and refines the estimation through shared dictionary learning.
PLSDA-batch estimates treatment and batch variation with latent components, then subtracts batch-associated components from microbiome data.
Citations: Wang Y, Lê Cao K-A. Brief Bioinform. 2023;24(2):bbac622.

Description: ConQuR removes microbiome batch effects with a two-part conditional quantile regression model and outputs batch-removed zero-inflated read counts.
MMUPHin provides normalization, statistical meta-analysis, and population-structure discovery for microbial taxonomic and functional profiles.
RUV-III-NB models raw sequencing counts with negative binomial or zero-inflated negative binomial distributions for count-data normalization.
Citations: Salim A et al. Nucleic Acids Res. 2022;50(16):e96.

Description: ComBat-seq uses negative binomial regression to estimate batch effects from count matrices and returns batch-adjusted count data.
Citations: Zhang Y, Parmigiani G, Johnson WE. NAR Genom Bioinform. 2020;2(3):lqaa078.

Description: FSQN applies feature-specific quantile normalization for cross-platform classification of molecular subtypes using gene-expression data.
Citations: Franks JM, Cai G, Whitfield ML. Bioinformatics. 2018;34(11):1868-1874.

Description: FAbatch is described as a model-based method for batch-effect correction in analyses involving a binary target variable.
Citations: Hornung R, Boulesteix A-L, Causeur D. BMC Bioinformatics. 2016;17:27.

Description: limma provides linear-model and empirical-Bayes methods for differential expression analyses of RNA-seq and microarray data; removeBatchEffect removes systematic batch or covariate variation from expression data.
Citations: Ritchie ME et al. Nucleic Acids Res. 2015;43(7):e47.

Description: The cited work addresses removal of dataset-specific systematic bias so gene-expression datasets can be combined for meta-analysis.
Citations: Sims AH et al. BMC Med Genomics. 2008;1:42.

Description: ComBat uses parametric or non-parametric empirical Bayes frameworks for adjusting known batch effects in genomic data.
Citations: Johnson WE, Li C, Rabinovic A. Biostatistics. 2007;8(1):118-127.
